title: Why I Still Use a Physical Notebook
hook: I built a 5-agent pipeline to automate my content, then I picked up a pen and started writing in a physical journal. Here’s why optimization is a trap.

title: Escaping the 12-Month Agent Lag
hook: If you’re still 'prompt engineering' a single agent, you’re building a legacy system. The 2026 stack is about stateless swarm coordination.

title: From 0% to 100%: The Triage Win
hook: Last week, my incident triage agent was hallucinating 40% of the root causes. This morning, it hit a 100% pass rate on the evaluation suite.

title: The Emergence Moment
hook: There is a specific second when 'I told it to' becomes 'it understood.'

title: The Swarm That Deployed Itself
hook: I just watched a team of 5 AI agents deploy themselves, update their own operating contracts, and then offer to QA their own work unprompted.

title: The Self-Optimizing Agent: End of Prompt Engineering
hook: I stopped 'prompt engineering' and started building agents that fix themselves. This is how the system actually learns from its own mistakes.
notes: Manually published by user.

title: The Struggle of Scale: When HNSW Breaks
hook: Local HNSW is a lie once you hit 100+ agents. I thought I had the memory substrate solved until I tried to scale the swarm.

title: Production RAG vs. Toys
hook: Forget demo-tier RAG. If you're not using distributed vector storage for your enterprise data, you're just playing with toys.
